beg.desc: C# runs on .NET. Every program starts with a Main method or top-level statements.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Understanding .NET program structure.

int.desc: Use top-level statements (C# 9+) and global using directives for cleaner code.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Minimal API programs with top-level statements.

pro.desc: Use file-scoped namespaces and nullable reference types for modern C# style.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Null-safe code with nullable reference types.

beg.desc: C# is strongly typed with primitive types: int, double, string, bool, char.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Storing typed data in applications.

int.desc: Use var for implicit typing and understand value types vs reference types.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Type inference and nullable values.

pro.desc: Use records for immutable data types and pattern matching with types.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Immutable domain models with records.

beg.desc: Use $ for string interpolation and common string methods.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Text formatting and validation.

int.desc: Use raw string literals (C# 11) and StringBuilder for efficient concatenation.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Embedded JSON templates and efficient string building.

pro.desc: Use Span<char> for zero-allocation string processing.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: High-performance string parsing in hot paths.

beg.desc: Arrays hold fixed-size collections. List<T> provides dynamic sizing.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Storing and iterating over data.

int.desc: Use Dictionary, HashSet, and LINQ for powerful collection operations.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Key-value lookups and deduplication.

pro.desc: Use ImmutableArray and Frozen collections (C# 11) for thread-safe read-only data.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Thread-safe configuration objects.

beg.desc: Use if/else and switch for decision-making.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Grading and access control.

int.desc: Use switch expressions with pattern matching for concise branching.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Type-safe dispatching with pattern matching.

pro.desc: Use list patterns and property patterns (C# 11) for advanced matching.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Complex business rule matching.

beg.desc: C# supports for, foreach, while, and do-while loops.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Iterating through collections.

int.desc: Use LINQ methods as functional loop alternatives.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Functional data transformation with LINQ.

pro.desc: Use yield return for custom lazy iterators that produce values on demand.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Lazy evaluation for infinite sequences.

beg.desc: Methods can have default parameters, named arguments, and return values.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Reusable logic blocks.

int.desc: Use out parameters, tuples, and expression-bodied members.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Functions with multiple return values.

pro.desc: Use in parameters for read-only references and ref struct for stack-only types.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: High-performance zero-allocation APIs.

beg.desc: Classes bundle data and behavior. Use properties for encapsulated access.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Object-oriented modeling.

int.desc: Use inheritance, interfaces, and sealed for controlled hierarchies.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Polymorphic rendering systems.

pro.desc: Use records, primary constructors (C# 12), and static abstract interface members.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Generic math and parsable types.

beg.desc: async/await handles asynchronous operations without blocking threads.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Non-blocking HTTP requests.

int.desc: Use Task.WhenAll for parallel async operations with error handling.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Fetching multiple APIs in parallel.

pro.desc: Use IAsyncEnumerable for streaming async data and CancellationToken for cancellation.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Streaming data with cancellation support.

beg.desc: LINQ queries collections using SQL-like syntax or method chains.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Sorting, filtering, and aggregating data.

int.desc: Use GroupBy, Join, and aggregate functions for complex queries.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Grouped analytics like SQL GROUP BY.

pro.desc: Use LINQ with expression trees for queryable providers (like EF Core).
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Database queries via LINQ-to-SQL in Entity Framework.

beg.desc: Generics let you create type-safe reusable code.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Type-safe generic containers.

int.desc: Use generic constraints and multiple type parameters.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Generic repository pattern in data access.

pro.desc: Use covariance/contravariance and static abstract members in generic interfaces.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Generic math operations across numeric types.

beg.desc: Use try/catch/finally for structured error handling.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Handling invalid user input.

int.desc: Create custom exceptions with inner exceptions for error chains.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Domain-specific error types with exception filters.

pro.desc: Use Result<T> pattern for error handling without exceptions.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Functional error handling without exception overhead.

beg.desc: Delegates are type-safe function pointers. Events use delegates for notifications.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Callback functions and event handlers.

int.desc: Use events for the observer pattern with proper encapsulation.
int.usecase: Real-world use: UI event handling in WPF/WinForms.

pro.desc: Use Func/Action composition and custom event args for rich event systems.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: ASP.NET Core-style middleware pipelines.

beg.desc: Properties provide controlled access to class fields.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Encapsulated data access.

int.desc: Use init-only properties and required members for immutable initialization.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Immutable configuration objects.

pro.desc: Use computed properties and indexers for domain-specific access patterns.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Matrix types with natural indexing syntax.

beg.desc: Use File class for simple file reading and writing.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Reading and writing text files.

int.desc: Use StreamReader/StreamWriter for large file processing.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Appending to log files.

pro.desc: Use System.IO.Pipelines for high-performance streaming I/O.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: High-throughput network protocol parsing.

beg.desc: Structs are value types stored on the stack — efficient for small data.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Lightweight coordinate types.

int.desc: Use readonly struct for immutable value types with no defensive copies.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Immutable value types for color and pixel data.

pro.desc: Use ref struct and Span<T> for zero-allocation stack-based processing.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Zero-allocation tokenizers for hot-path parsing.

beg.desc: Enable nullable warnings to prevent null reference exceptions at compile time.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Preventing NullReferenceException at compile time.

int.desc: Use null-forgiving operator and MemberNotNull attributes.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Guaranteed initialization with nullable analysis.

pro.desc: Use nullable annotations in generic code and API design.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Nullable-aware generic extension methods.

beg.desc: Interfaces define contracts. Abstract classes provide partial implementations.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Polymorphic shapes through interfaces.

int.desc: Use default interface methods for backward-compatible API evolution.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Adding methods to interfaces without breaking implementations.

pro.desc: Use generic interface constraints and static abstract members.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Generic math with static abstract interface members.

beg.desc: DI provides dependencies to classes instead of having them create their own.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Loose coupling between components.

int.desc: Use Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection for IoC container.
int.usecase: Real-world use: ASP.NET Core dependency injection.

pro.desc: Use keyed services and service descriptors for advanced DI scenarios.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Named service resolution in microservices.

beg.desc: EF Core maps C# classes to database tables automatically.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Object-relational mapping for databases.

int.desc: Use migrations, relationships, and LINQ queries with EF Core.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Database queries with eager loading.

pro.desc: Use raw SQL, compiled queries, and bulk operations for performance.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Optimized database operations in production.

beg.desc: ASP.NET Core builds web APIs and applications with minimal code.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Minimal API web applications.

int.desc: Use controllers, middleware, and model binding for structured APIs.
int.usecase: Real-world use: RESTful API controllers.

pro.desc: Use middleware pipeline, filters, and minimal API groups.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Request timing middleware and route grouping.

beg.desc: Tasks represent asynchronous operations. Use Task.Run for CPU-bound work.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Running background computations.

int.desc: Use SemaphoreSlim and channels for concurrent coordination.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Rate-limiting concurrent operations.

pro.desc: Use System.Threading.Channels for producer-consumer patterns.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: High-throughput producer-consumer queues.

beg.desc: Records provide value-based equality and immutable data types.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Immutable data transfer objects.

int.desc: Use record structs for stack-allocated immutable values.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Value objects in domain-driven design.

pro.desc: Use records for discriminated unions with exhaustive matching.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Algebraic data types with pattern matching.

beg.desc: Span<T> provides safe access to contiguous memory without allocation.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Stack-allocated arrays for performance.

int.desc: Use Span for zero-copy string and array slicing.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Zero-copy CSV parsing.

pro.desc: Use Memory<T> for async-compatible span operations and ArrayPool for buffer reuse.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: High-performance I/O with pooled buffers.

beg.desc: Source generators auto-generate code at compile time.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: AOT-friendly JSON serialization.

int.desc: Use regex source generators for compiled regex patterns.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Compile-time regex compilation for performance.

pro.desc: Create custom source generators using IIncrementalGenerator.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Auto-generating boilerplate code at compile time.
